description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: census-designated place in Franklin County, Tennessee
description_wiki: Sewanee () is a census-designated place (CDP) in Franklin County, Tennessee, United States.

description: US Marine Corps base near Oceanside, California, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton is the major West Coast base of the United States Marine Corps and is one of the largest Marine Corps bases in the United States.

description: city in San Diego County, California, United States
description_wiki: Oceanside is a beach city in the North County area of San Diego County, California, United States.

description: active United States Marine Corps Reserve formation
description_wiki: The 4th Marine Division is a reserve division in the United States Marine Corps.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: in the Marshall Islands, previously the two separate islands Roi and Namur, now joined by landfill
description_wiki: Roi-Namur ( roy nə-MOOR) is an island in the north part of the Kwajalein Atoll in the Marshall Islands.

description: Pacific atoll, part of the Marshall Islands
description_wiki: Kwajalein Atoll (; Marshallese: Kuwajleen [kʷuwɑzʲ(ɛ)lʲɛːnʲ]) is part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI).

description: 1949 original cast recording starring Mary Martin, Ezio Pinza
harbor on the island of Oahu, Hawaii, USA
description_wiki: Pearl Harbor is a lagoon harbor on the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States, west of Honolulu.

description: 1944 battle of the Pacific campaign of World War II on the island of Saipan in the Mariana Islands
description_wiki: The Battle of Saipan was an amphibious assault launched by the United States against the Empire of Japan during the Pacific campaign of World War II between 15 June and 9 July 1944.

description: 1944 battle of the Pacific campaign of World War II on the island of Tinian in the Mariana Islands
description_wiki: The Battle of Tinian was part of the Pacific campaign of World War II. It was fought between the United States and Japan on the island of Tinian in the Mariana Islands from 24 July until 1 August 1944.

description: 1945 major battle in which US Forces captured the island of Iwo Jima from Japan during World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Iwo Jima (硫黄島の戦い, Iōtō no Tatakai, Iōjima no Tatakai; 19 February – 26 March 1945) was a major battle in which the United States Marine Corps (USMC) and United States Navy (USN) landed on and eventually captured the island of Iwo Jima from the Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) during World War II. The American invasion, designated Operation Detachment, had the goal of capturing the island with its two airfields: South Field and Central Field.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: A Presidential Unit Citation is a unit citation awarded by the president of a state.

description: United States military decoration
description_wiki: The Purple Heart (PH) is a United States military decoration awarded in the name of the president to those wounded or killed while serving on or after 5 April 1917, with the U.S. military.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: second-largest of the Hawaiian Islands, in the United States
description_wiki: Maui is the second-largest island in the Hawaiian archipelago, at 727.2 square miles (1,883 km2).

description: city in and county seat of Honolulu County, Hawaii, United States, that is also the capital city of Hawaii
description_wiki: Honolulu (  HON-ə-LOO-loo; Hawaiian: [honoˈlulu]) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Hawaii, located in the Pacific Ocean.

aliases: The Royal Hawaiian, a Luxury Collection Resort, Waikiki
description: hotel in Waikīkī, Honolulu, Oʻahu, Hawaii, USA
description_wiki: The Royal Hawaiian Hotel is a beachfront luxury hotel located in Waikiki in Honolulu, Hawaii, on the island of Oahu.

description: landing craft
city in and county seat of Hamilton County, Tennessee, United States
description_wiki: Chattanooga ( CHAT-ə-NOO-gə) is a city in Hamilton County, Tennessee, United States, and its county seat.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: US Marine Corps base in Jacksonville, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune ( lə-ZHURN or  lə-ZHOON) is a 246-square-mile (640 km2) United States military training facility in Jacksonville, North Carolina.

description: liberal arts university in Sewanee, Tennessee
description_wiki: The University of the South, familiarly known as Sewanee (), is a private Episcopal liberal arts college in Sewanee, Tennessee, United States.

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description: World War II entertainment venue for military personnel
description_wiki: The Hollywood Canteen operated at 1451 North Cahuenga Boulevard in the Los Angeles, California, neighborhood of Hollywood between October 3, 1942, and November 22, 1945, as a club offering food, dancing, and entertainment for enlisted men and women, who were usually on their way overseas during World War II. Even though the majority of visitors were US servicemen, the canteen was open to allied countries as well as women in all branches of service.

description: American actor (1920–2014)
description_wiki: Mickey Rooney (born Ninnian Joseph Yule Jr., September 23, 1920 – April 6, 2014) was an American actor.

description: Dominican actress (1912-1951)
description_wiki: María África Gracia Vidal (6 June 1912 – 7 September 1951), known professionally as Maria Montez, was a Dominican actress who gained fame and popularity in the 1940s starring in a series of filmed-in-Technicolor costume adventure films.

description: American big band leader and musician
description_wiki: Thomas Francis Dorsey Jr.
